diagrams.title: C++ File Stream Class Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the inheritance relationship between C++ stream classes and file stream classes (ifstream, ofstream, fstream), helping learners understand how file I/O extends the standard stream functionality.
elements: ios base class (with failbit, badbit, eofbit flags)
ifstream class (input file stream - derived from istream)
ofstream class (output file stream - derived from ostream)
fstream class (bidirectional file stream - derived from iostream)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the object-oriented structure of file streams in C++ and their relationship to standard input/output streams

diagrams.title: File Operations Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: A step-by-step workflow showing the typical sequence of operations when reading from or writing to a file in C++, including error handling checkpoints.
elements: Error handling branch showing failure messages
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the proper sequence of file operations and where to check for errors during file I/O

diagrams.title: File Open Modes Reference
diagrams.description: A visual reference chart showing all C++ file open modes (ios::in, ios::out, ios::app, ios::ate, ios::binary, ios::trunc) and their effects on file reading/writing.
elements: Mode name labels (in, out, app, ate, binary, trunc)
Visual distinction between input and output modes
diagrams.purpose: Provides a quick visual reference for understanding what each file open mode does and when to use each one
